title: Creating Claude Agent Skills: From SKILL.md to MCPB

lessons.title: Progressive Disclosure and the Context Window
Advanced Authoring: Files, Scripts, and Evals
Where Skills Run, and Packaging as Plugins

covered: What an Agent Skill is and why it differs from a prompt
Progressive disclosure and the three loading levels
SKILL.md frontmatter rules, naming, and writing descriptions that trigger
Authoring craft: conciseness, degrees of freedom, bundled files, utility scripts, workflows, and evaluation-driven development
Where Skills run (claude.ai, Claude Code, API) and their runtime constraints
Claude Code's extensions to the standard (invocation control, allowed-tools, context: fork, dynamic injection)
Packaging and distributing skills as Claude Code plugins and via marketplaces
MCPB bundles: the manifest.json, server types, user_config, the mcpb CLI, and how MCPB relates to Skills and Plugins

excluded.topic: Building an MCP server from scratch (protocol internals, transports, SDK APIs)
excluded.reason: Out of the mission's scope; the course covers only what packaging an existing server requires. See the modelcontextprotocol/mcpb examples and MCP docs.

excluded.topic: Full mechanics of the Claude API Skills endpoints and the Claude Agent SDK
excluded.reason: Deliberately out of scope per the mission; the course states where skills are uploaded but does not walk the /v1/skills API in depth.

excluded.topic: A complete runnable end-to-end MCPB example project
excluded.reason: No single Tier 1/2 source provides one inline; the manifest and CLI are taught accurately and the course points to the official mcpb examples directory.
